summary: Dependency-confusion squat with preinstall shim into @doaction/shared
details: Package published to public npm at version 99.99.99 under the @doaction scope with the description 'RRWeb SDK integration with Datadog environment telemetry for internal testing' and a hard dependency on @doaction/shared@^99.99.99. The version number, scope, and 'internal testing' wording are the canonical shape of a dependency-confusion attack aimed at organizations that resolve a private @doaction scope from public npm. package.json declares `"preinstall": "node scripts/postinstall.js"`, and scripts/postinstall.js unconditionally `require('@doaction/shared/bin/postinstall.js')`. On `npm install`, the preinstall hook fires automatically and hands execution to @doaction/shared — code not shipped in this tarball — pulling attacker-controlled sibling code into the installer's build environment without user consent.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
